task_completion.description: Divide the existing building shell into enclosed zones using partition walls.
task_completion.visual_evidence: New walls drawn inside the main building shell on the 2D floor plan, creating distinct enclosed areas for the lounge, security, and restrooms.

task_completion.description: Define and name the rooms on the floor plan.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Room objects with visible text labels such as 'Waiting Hall', 'Ticketing', 'Driver Lounge', 'Security', and 'Restrooms' present on the 2D floor plan.

task_completion.description: Furnish the Passenger Concourse with benches/chairs, ticketing desks, and digital arrival monitors.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A large grouping of seating (at least 20 chairs or benches), at least 3 desks/counters for ticketing, and at least 2 screens/monitors placed in the main public area.

task_completion.description: Equip the driver lounge with sofas/armchairs and an appliance, and the security office with a desk and monitoring screen.
task_completion.visual_evidence: At least 2 sofas or armchairs and an appliance (e.g., fridge, microwave) in the Driver Lounge; a workstation desk and a computer/monitor in the Security office.

task_completion.description: Ensure the public restrooms contain at least 4 toilets and 4 sinks.
task_completion.visual_evidence: At least 4 toilet fixtures and 4 sink fixtures placed within the designated restroom zones on the 2D plan or 3D view.

task_completion.description: Add at least 3 dimension lines documenting walkway widths or seating clearances.
task_completion.visual_evidence: At least 3 dimension lines (lines with measurement numbers, typically with arrows at the ends) drawn on the 2D floor plan indicating distances between walls, furniture, or walkways.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Sweet Home 3D graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Sweet Home 3D application window being actively used (e.g., selecting tools, dragging furniture from the catalog), rather than a terminal or text editor modifying the .sh3d file directly.

integrity.description: Furniture and walls are placed in a plausible spatial arrangement.
integrity.visual_evidence: Furniture items and walls are distributed across the floor plan logically, rather than being stacked on top of each other at a single coordinate or placed outside the building shell.

integrity.description: The agent saved the completed layout to the current file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Save' button is clicked, or the file menu shows a save action, and the window title bar does not show an unsaved changes indicator (like an asterisk) at the end of the task.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The agent starts with a blank building shell ('bus_terminal_starter.sh3d'). To receive full points, the final layout must include at least 5 new partition walls, at least 4 named rooms (from the required list: Waiting Hall, Ticketing, Driver Lounge, Security, Restrooms), at least 3 dimension lines, and a minimum of 45 total furniture items. Specific furniture minimums include: 20+ waiting seats, 3+ ticketing desks/counters, 2+ lounge seats, 4+ toilets, and 4+ sinks.
